parsed_review.summary: CP00-924 is a descriptor-only RP30 embedded-HRX bridge pack (CandidateApi tail S03-S05, EvidenceApi S01-S05, HROperations S01-S02; 10 units, risk A). It does not claim production_ready (validation-report, manifest review block, construction-inspection all show production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / status pending_review), and its only positive claim is descriptor verification. Direct inspection confirms the claim is supported: HRX_CP924_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:542) chains consistently CP923->CP924->CP925 and matches the manifest/baseline range and 10-unit distribution; HRX_CP924_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:1986) and manifest micro_title_distribution match (CandidateApi 3 / EvidenceApi 5 / HROperations 2). The row factory (registry.js:2078) sets every runtime/record-write/payload/policy-execution/finalization/real-data/secret flag to false, and validateHrxCp924CandidateEvidenceHrOperationsBridgeDescriptor (validators.js:4776) actively rejects any non-false boundary field, missing fields, non-read-only Claude packet, runtime-receipt emission, enterprise-trust claim, or handoff/capability/safety-gate/no-leak-guard drift. Authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false (Hermes used only for deterministic review/evidence packet handling, not product runtime). Tests (model.test.js:1482-1560) assert these boundaries meaningfully; the generated contract JSON matches the projection with validation.valid=true and no real-data/secret leakage. Review-hardening checks pass: prompt 2419B < 25600B limit, no full source/diff inlined, schema present, tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob, no forbidden tools, auth preflight exit 0 with valid JSON result. No P0/P1/P2 findings. One P3: command-evidence.json captures only the HRX-specific deterministic subset (6 commands, all exit 0), not the full 10-command closeout ladder named in the prompt; the pack notes and adjudication.md explicitly defer that ladder to the post-promotion run before commit and keep production_ready=no until then, so closeout remains correctly gated. As a read-only reviewer (Bash prohibited) I could not execute that ladder. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not the final approver; the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so neither pack nor goal closeout is blocked by this review.

findings.title: Full closeout validation ladder not present in pre-review command evidence
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only the HRX-specific deterministic subset (node --check on registry/validators/validate script, node --test on hrx tests, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate) with exit_code 0 each. The broader 10-command closeout ladder named in the review prompt (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not captured in the evidence at review time. The artifact notes state these pre-review checks 'must be re-confirmed by the post-promotion validation ladder before commit,' and adjudication.md keeps production_ready=no until that full ladder completes. As a read-only reviewer with Bash prohibited, I cannot independently execute or confirm those commands.
findings.recommendation: Before promoting/committing CP00-924, run and capture the full deterministic validation ladder; ensure all pass and git diff --check is clean, and do not flip production_ready to true until then. Optionally append the ladder results to command-evidence.json for an auditable post-review record.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected sequencing per pack notes and adjudication.md (review -> adjudication -> queue regen -> full ladder -> commit); not an implementation defect. Non-blocking for this review. Closeout correctly remains gated on the full ladder.
